[[Paul Tierney (referee)|Paul Tierney]] (stepfather) }} }} '''Lewis Tierney''' (born 20 October 1994) is a former [[rugby league]] footballer who played on the {{rlp|wg}} or at {{rlp|fb}}. He started his professional career in 2013 with the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Super League]], and spent time on loan at [[Workington Town]]. He joined the [[Catalans Dragons]] in 2017, and played for them in the 2018 [[Challenge Cup]] final. He played for [[Leigh Centurions]] for one season before announcing his retirement in 2021. Tierney was also a [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] international, and was capped six times between 2016 and 2018. ==Early life== Tierney was born in [[Wigan]], Greater Manchester, England and is the eldest son of [[Jason Robinson (rugby)|Jason Robinson]]. His parents separated shortly after his birth, and his mother remarried [[Paul Tierney (referee)|Paul Tierney]].{{cite web|last1=Wilson|first1=Andy|title=Jason Robinson's son Lewis Tierney signs with Wigan Warriors|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2012/oct/23/lewis-tierney-jason-robinson-wigan-warriors|website=The Guardian|accessdate=21 July 2014|date=23 October 2012}} His brother [[Patrick Robinson (cyclist)|Patrick]] is a professional cyclist. ==Club career== ===Wigan Warriors=== Tierney made his Super League début for Wigan in June 2013, scoring a try in a 33–32 victory over [[Widnes Vikings]].{{cite web|title=Super League: Widnes Vikings 32-33 Wigan Warriors|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/22768872|website=BBC Sport|access-date=21 July 2014|date=7 June 2013}} In 2014 and 2015, Tierney spent time on dual registration at Workington Town where he made 25 appearances scoring 6 tries in his time in Cumbria. In 2016 Tierney won his first major piece of silverware after Wigan beat Warrington in the Super League Grand Final; he became the first son to follow his father's footsteps and win a Grand Final.{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2016/oct/08/super-league-grand-final-warrington-wigan-live|title=Warrington 6-12 Wigan: Super League Grand Final – as it happened!|publisher=Guardian|date=8 October 2016|accessdate=6 November 2019}}{{citation needed|date=April 2026}} In July 2017, Tierney was loaned to [[Catalans Dragons]] until the end of the season, where he played in their [[Million Pound Game]] victory over Leigh to stay in Super League.{{cite web|title=Romain Navarrete and Lewis Tierney: Wigan Warriors loan duo to Catalans Dragons|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/40595017|website=BBC Sport|accessdate=18 August 2017|date=13 July 2017}} ===Catalans Dragons=== In October 2017, Tierney signed a two-year deal at Catalans after a successful loan period in 2017.{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/41686482|title=Lewis Tierney: Scotland back joins Catalans Dragons from Wigan Warriors|publisher=BBC Sport|date=19 October 2017|accessdate=24 January 2018}} He played in the [[2018 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over the [[Warrington Wolves]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2018/aug/25/catalans-dragons-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Catalans Dragons beat Warrington in Challenge Cup final to make history|publisher=Guardian|date=25 August 2018|accessdate=5 November 2019}} ===Leigh Centurions=== On 26 December 2020 it was announced that Tierney had signed for the [[Leigh Centurions]] for the 2021 season.{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=26 Dec 2020|accessdate=26 Dec 2020|title=Lewis Tierney: Leigh sign ex-Wigan back from Catalans Dragons|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55452620}} Tierney announced his retirement in November 2021.{{cite web |title=Lewis Tierney announces retirement |url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36317/lewis-tierney-announces-retirement |publisher=The Rugby Football League |access-date=5 July 2022 |date=26 November 2021}} ==International career== Via his heritage, Tierney is eligible to represent both [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]]{{cite web|last=Smith|first=Duncan|date=19 October 2016|title=Wigan RL star Tierney: I'll be proud to pull on Scots jersey|url=https://www.scotsman.com/sport/more-in-sport/wigan-rl-star-tierney-i-ll-be-proud-to-pull-on-scots-jersey-1-4262461|publisher=The Scotsman|accessdate=20 February 2018}} and [[Jamaica national rugby league team|Jamaica]] through his paternal grandmother and paternal grandfather, respectively. Tierney made his international debut for Scotland in the [[2016 Rugby League Four Nations|2016 Four Nations]]. He played in two of Scotland's games and scored one try. ==Honours== *[[Super League]]: 2016 *[[Million Pound Game]]: 2017 *[[World Club Challenge]]: 2017 *[[Challenge Cup]]: 2018 ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category}} *[http://www.catalansdragons.com/en/fiche-441-tierney-lewis/ Catalans Dragons profile] *[http://www.wiganwarriors.com/teams/first-team/lewis-tierney Wigan Warriors profile] *[https://www.superleague.co.uk/player/18/lewis%3Cbr-%3Etierney SL profile] *[http://www.rlwc2017.com/players?team=383#Lewis-Tierney 2017 RLWC profile] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171107055149/http://www.rlwc2017.com/players?team=383#Lewis-Tierney |date=7 November 2017 }} {{Wigan Warriors squad - 2016 Super League champions}} {{Wigan Warriors - 2017 World Club Challenge winners}} {{Catalans Dragons - 2018 Challenge Cup Final winners}} {{Scotland 2016 Rugby League Four Nations squad}} {{Scotland 2017 Rugby League World Cup squad}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tierney, Lewis}} [[Category:1994 births]] [[Category:Living people]] [[Category:Catalans Dragons players]] [[Category:English rugby league players]] [[Category:English people of Jamaican descent]] [[Category:Sportspeople of Jamaican descent]] [[Category:English people of Scottish descent]] [[Category:Leigh Leopards players]] [[Category:Rugby league fullbacks]] [[Category:Rugby league wingers]] [[Category:Rugby league players from Wigan]] [[Category:Scotland national rugby league team players]] [[Category:Wigan Warriors players]] [[Category:Workington Town players]] {{England-rugbyleague-bio-stub}}
